specific_location: Approx. 15 miles southwest of Saguache. From Saguache, travel south on HWY 285 about 6 mi; turn right on County Road T. Follow about 1.5 mi; then bear right following signs for Tracy Canyon and N. Tracy Canyon. Continue about 1.4 mi. Turn right on unimproved road at N. Tracy Canyon sign. Continue on N. Tracy Canyon Road about 4.5 mi., then turn left. Follow about 2 mi, winding through pinyon trees, until a FS gate. Go through the gate and continue to drive or walks up the road, about to 1/2 mi or so.
description: The bird's body was small and warbler-shaped---horizontal oriented body, thin pointed bill, tail not longer than it's body. Its plumage was all black and white---black and white striped crown with white eye stripe; black lores, ear patch, and throat; white underparts with heavy black streaking on flanks; and two distinctive white wing bars. Plumage was similar to Black-throated Gray Warbler; however, this bird did not have a yellow spot in front of its eye as does BTYW.Behaviors: At first, I did not see the bird but heard it singing spontaneously within some deciduous shrubs near a small aspen stand within a larger mixed-conifer-aspen landscape. The bird then popped up out of the shrubs and landed on a low branch in a nearby Douglas fir tree and continued singing. The bird was about 20 feet from me so I got a fantastic view. I watched the bird for several minutes as it continued singing. It did not interact with any other birds while I observed it. I tried to move closer to the bird and it began to move away from me. I continued to pursue the bird, and it eventually stopped singing and flew out the the general area.Call: wee-see-wee-see-wee-see (squeaky wheel)Plumage: Breeding
